---
title: "Pigmentation gene-network build"
---

An expanded, gene-level pigmentation network — built from the Raghunath (2015) directed model plus
pigmentation genes discovered since — used to produce a network-derived, ranked list of candidate modifier
genes ("GWAS by node") that explains why genotype→phenotype prediction succeeds for some people and fails
for others.

Pigmentation is the instrument because its environmental variance is near-zero and its heritability is high,
so discordance is almost purely a genetic-architecture phenomenon — a clean model system for the
genotype→phenotype map.

## The finding

A signed directed network that explains genotype→phenotype discordance in **both directions** with the same
machinery:

- **D1 — genotype without phenotype:** a causal variant is present but modifier-node states block the path
  to the pigment endpoint (incomplete penetrance / epistasis).
- **D2 — phenotype without the usual genotype:** the phenotype is present but the catalogued causal variant
  is absent — the endpoint is reachable by alternative routes.

## This site

- **[Case-set provenance](docs/NB3_case_assembly_provenance.qmd)** — how the 13-paper validation-case set
  was acquired, extracted, and direction-classified (694 records; 3 D1 / 5 D2 / 5 both). Authored as a frozen
  Quarto document: the extractor code is shown for provenance but does not run on render.

Authoritative project documents live in the repository: `README.md` (public rebuild guide),
`project_dashboard.md` (internal control surface), `DATA_SOURCES.md` (provenance manifest). Data provenance
for every node and edge traces to a recorded source.
